channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trial hears ex-footballer denies direct role in stabbing of Iranian journalist, blaming alleged accomplice
item.description: A former professional Romanian footballer has told a London court that he was not the person who stabbed an Iranian journalist in a suspected politically motivated attack. Nandito Badea, 21, who previ...

item.title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani Defends Sanctuary City Status Against Homeland Security Threats
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani strongly responded to comments made by Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin regarding potential penalties for sanctuary cities. Mullin had stated that his de...

item.title: Tanzania and WaterAid UK strengthen partnership to improve water and sanitation services
item.description: Tanzania and WaterAid UK have pledged to deepen their cooperation to improve water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) services across the country. This agreement was reached during the African Developmen...

item.title: Canada Post posts $205 million quarterly loss amid labour uncertainty and restructuring efforts
item.description: Canada Post has reported a pre-tax loss of $205 million for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting ongoing financial difficulties as the Crown corporation continues to face declining mail and parcel vo...

item.title: Themba Zwane’s leadership tested in Bafana Bafana’s final World Cup friendly
item.description: As Bafana Bafana gear up for the Fifa World Cup next month, their final warm-up match at Orlando Stadium ended in a goalless draw against Nicaragua, leaving supporters slightly disappointed. The match...

item.title: Malacañang declares special non-working holidays in six Philippine areas for June commemorations
item.description: Malacañang has announced the declaration of special non-working holidays in six areas across the Philippines in June to allow residents to participate in local commemorations and festivities. Accordin...

item.title: Bauchi APM Sets 2027 Election Agenda: Fair Play and Strong Campaign
item.description: Governor Bala Mohammed of Bauchi State don talk say Allied People’s Movement (APM) no go do any form of election rigging and nor go allow anybody cheat dem for 2027 general elections. Dis statement co...

item.title: Mike Defensor denies quid pro quo in P75-million campaign donation linked to Marcoleta case
item.description: Former AnaKalusugan Representative Mike Defensor has denied allegations that he asked for or received any favors in exchange for a campaign donation he made to Senator Rodante Marcoleta during the 202...

item.title: GOP adviser says Trump is increasingly isolated and acting like a lame-duck president amid focus on retribution
item.description: The article discusses internal Republican concerns about President Donald Trump’s current political influence and strategic direction during the 2026 election cycle. According to a GOP Senate adviser ...

item.title: Interior B.C. communities experience record-breaking temperatures in late May
item.description: Several communities across British Columbia's Interior, including Kelowna, have recently experienced record-setting temperatures, marking a significant heatwave in the region. Meteorologists reported ...

item.title: Supreme Court orders review of Mississippi death row conviction over alleged jury selection racial bias
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court issued a narrow 5-4 ruling in a case involving Terry Pitchford, a Mississippi man who has been on death row for approximately two decades following his conviction for the murder...

item.title: American Fugitive Charged with Sexual Assault to Appear in Gqeberha Court
item.description: American national Robert Lee Redd junior, 56, wanted for alleged sexual assaults on two minors in Texas in 2017 and 2019, has been arrested in Westbrook, Gqeberha. His arrest followed a two-year inter...

item.title: Rajesh Kumar Singh assigned additional charge of DRDO leadership following Samir Kamat’s retirement
item.description: Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh has been given the additional responsibility of serving as the Chairman of the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) as well as Secretary of the Dep...

item.title: FIVB suspends Philippine National Volleyball Federation due to governance and ethics concerns
item.description: The Fédération Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB) has announced the immediate suspension of the Philippine National Volleyball Federation (PNVF) following what it described as serious governance conc...

item.title: Nigerian Navy expands training and infrastructure to strengthen security operations
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has stepped up efforts to improve its training capacity and infrastructure in order to respond more effectively to emerging security threats and enhance operational efficiency across...

item.title: SACP hosts conference to unite leftist organisations despite absentees
item.description: The South African Communist Party (SACP) has launched its first 'conference of the left' at the Birchwood Hotel and Conference Centre in Boksburg. The gathering brings together political formations, c...

item.title: Utumishi Girls dorm fire: witness recalls two girls dying while clinging to each other
item.description: An eyewitness in Gilgil has described the traumatic moments during the Utumishi Girls Academy dorm fire, detailing how first responders faced a locked exit door that hindered rescue efforts. Stephen M...

item.title: Dappa Man Opens Up About Seven Years in Jail and Life Lessons
item.description: Dappa Man, a rising content creator and member of Oga Obinna’s team, emotionally opened up about spending seven years behind bars. He revealed that his arrest was linked to false accusations from his ...

item.title: THRDC urges police and Maasai leaders cooperation to ensure fair investigation in Bhaozang Ge murder case
item.description: Human rights advocates together with Maasai community leaders in Dar es Salaam have called for closer cooperation with the Police Force in the investigation of the killing of Chinese factory owner Bha...

item.title: Teenager João Fonseca defeats Novak Djokovic in five-set French Open thriller
item.description: Novak Djokovic’s pursuit of a record 25th Grand Slam title came to a dramatic halt at the French Open after a stunning third-round defeat to 19-year-old Brazilian João Fonseca. In a gripping five-set ...
